title: olea ferruginea
intro: Olea ferruginea, also known as ironwood or rusty olive, is a species of flowering plant in the family Oleaceae. It is native to the Mediterranean region and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant's extracts have been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, wounds, and skin conditions, due to its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Olea ferruginea can be prepared into herbal teas, capsules, or tinctures, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
